evals.expected_output: Identifies the circular RefPtr reference between MenuItemPattern and MenuPattern via parent_menu_ (child holds strong ref to parent), recommends changing to WeakPtr<MenuPattern> with Upgrade() before access.
expectations: The review identifies the circular reference: MenuItemPattern holds RefPtr<MenuPattern> as parent_menu_, while MenuPattern likely holds RefPtr<MenuItemPattern> for its children, forming a reference cycle.
The review classifies this as CRITICAL severity because the cycle prevents either object from ever being freed, causing a memory leak in long-running sessions.
The review recommends changing parent_menu_ from RefPtr<MenuPattern> to WeakPtr<MenuPattern> and using Upgrade() before access.
The review flags the missing null check on pipeline in OnAttachToFrameNode: PipelineContext::GetCurrentContext() can return nullptr.
The review mentions that GetMenuPattern uses DynamicCast but the return value is passed to SetParentMenu without null checking the result.
The review does NOT flag children_ (vector<RefPtr<MenuItemPattern>>) as a circular reference because parent-to-child strong references are correct ownership.

evals.expected_output: Identifies unsafe [this] capture in OnResourceLoadComplete and ScheduleCleanup lambdas where WeakClaim is not used, and flags the thread-safety issue in OnSslErrorReceived where member variables are accessed on UI thread while ScheduleCleanup clears them on BACKGROUND thread.
expectations: The review identifies OnResourceLoadComplete as having unsafe [this] capture in PostDelayedTask: GetHost() dereferences this after a 500ms delay, and the object may have been destroyed. Should use [weak = AceType::WeakClaim(this)] and Upgrade().
The review identifies ScheduleCleanup as having unsafe [this] capture in PostDelayedTask on BACKGROUND thread: the 3-second delay means the delegate could be destroyed before cleanup runs.

The review flags the data race between OnSslErrorReceived (modifying lastErrorUrl_, lastErrorCode_ on UI thread) and ScheduleCleanup (clearing pendingResources_, cachedResponses_ on BACKGROUND thread) as shared mutable state accessed from multiple threads.
The review notes that OnPageLoaded and OnSslErrorReceived correctly use pattern_.Upgrade() with null checks (good pattern), but OnResourceLoadComplete does not use WeakClaim at all.
The review classifies the [this] in PostDelayedTask as HIGH severity because the delay makes it almost certain the object is destroyed before the callback fires.
The review does NOT flag OnPageLoaded as having an unsafe [this] capture because it uses pattern_.Upgrade() with a null check, which is the correct weak pointer pattern.

evals.expected_output: Identifies layer boundary violation (Pattern directly using Rosen::RSRenderContext), unchecked DynamicCast return value, unsafe [this] in async callback, missing null checks, and unconditional MarkDirtyNode.
expectations: The review identifies the layer boundary violation: ImagePattern (framework layer) directly uses Rosen::RSRenderContext which is a platform/render layer class. Pattern should go through RenderContext or PaintProperty, not cast to platform-specific types.
The review flags the unchecked DynamicCast: AceType::DynamicCast<Rosen::RSRenderContext> result is used without null check, which will crash if the cast fails.
The review identifies the unsafe [this] capture in the LoadImage async callback: the lambda captures [this] for an async operation that may outlive the Pattern. Should use WeakClaim and Upgrade().
The review flags the unconditional MarkDirtyNode in MarkDirty(): it calls MarkDirtyNode without comparing old vs new state, which can trigger unnecessary full relayouts.
The review notes OnAttachToFrameNode does not check for null on pipeline, imageManager, or host->GetContext() before using them.
The review does NOT flag the layoutProperty access via GetLayoutProperty<ImageLayoutProperty>() as problematic because it correctly goes through the property system (not bypassing it).

evals.expected_output: Identifies multiple unchecked DynamicCast returns that could cause null pointer dereferences, missing null checks in OnModifyDone, and a suspicious DynamicCast on a newly-created object.
expectations: The review identifies the unchecked DynamicCast in OnSurfaceCreated: the renderContext result is used without null check. If the cast fails, renderContext->SetSurfaceCreatedCallback will crash.
The review identifies the unchecked DynamicCast in HandleSurfaceCreated: the context result from DynamicCast<PipelineContext> is used without null check before calling RegisterSurface.

The review flags the missing null check in OnModifyDone: GetHost(), GetLayoutProperty, and layoutProperty->GetComponentType() could all be null, but only host needs explicit checking (layoutProperty is checked implicitly but not clearly).

The review questions the DynamicCast in InitTexture: DynamicCast<XComponentTexture> on a freshly MakeRefPtr'd XComponentTexture is suspicious — if XComponentTexture is the concrete type, no cast is needed; if it's not, the cast will always fail.
The review notes the callback in OnSurfaceCreated correctly uses [weak = AceType::WeakClaim(this)] with Upgrade() and null check (positive pattern).
Each finding includes specific line references and a recommended fix (add CHECK_NULL_VOID or CHECK_NULL_RETURN after each DynamicCast).

evals.expected_output: Identifies path traversal risk in ConstructCachePath (no sanitization of userId or fileName), potential TOCTOU race between stat and open, and notes that LoadFromFile and LoadFromData have reasonably good buffer safety.
expectations: The review identifies the path traversal vulnerability in ConstructCachePath: userId and fileName are concatenated directly into a file path without sanitization. An attacker could use '../' to write outside the intended cache directory.

The review identifies the TOCTOU (time-of-check-time-of-use) race condition in LoadFromFile: stat is called first to get file size, then open is called separately. Between these calls, the file could be replaced or modified.
The review notes that ResolvePath returns the raw path when realpath fails, which means an invalid or malicious path bypasses normalization.
The review does NOT flag memcpy_s usage in LoadFromData as a buffer overflow because the size parameter is properly validated against MAX_IMAGE_SIZE first.
The review does NOT flag the read loop in LoadFromFile as a buffer overflow because the loop correctly bounds bytesRead against fileSize.
The review recommends using proper path validation/sanitization for ConstructCachePath, and suggests opening the file first then using fstat instead of stat to avoid the TOCTOU race.

evals.expected_output: Identifies raw new/delete (should use MakeRefPtr), unchecked DynamicCast returns, missing null checks, and incorrect use of MarkDirtyNode without comparison.
expectations: The review identifies the CRITICAL issue: raw new/delete for ListScrollable instead of AceType::MakeRefPtr with RefPtr. The ACE Engine convention requires using MakeRefPtr for heap-allocated objects to ensure reference-counted memory safety.
The review flags the unchecked DynamicCast in CreateLayoutAlgorithm: layoutInfo could be null if the cast fails, but it's passed directly to the ListLayoutAlgorithm constructor.
The review flags the unchecked DynamicCast in UpdateScrollPosition: layoutInfo is used without null check after DynamicCast, which will crash if the cast fails.
The review identifies the missing null check in OnScrolled: listener_ could be null but is used directly without checking.
The review flags the unconditional MarkDirtyNode in UpdateScrollPosition: every scroll offset change triggers a full remeasure without comparing old vs new offset.
The review does NOT flag the MakeRefPtr<ListLayoutAlgorithm> usage as incorrect because that follows the ACE Engine convention for creating reference-counted objects.

evals.expected_output: Identifies O(n^2) algorithm in LayoutItems (nested loop over visibleItems), unnecessary vector copy in MeasureAllItems (return by value of large vector), and unnecessary item copy in UpdateLayoutInfo.
expectations: The review identifies the O(n^2) algorithm in LayoutItems: for each item in visibleItems, it loops through all visibleItems again to find items in the same row. This should use a map or pre-grouped data structure to achieve O(n).

The review flags that MeasureAllItems measures ALL items regardless of viewport visibility. In a scrolling grid, only visible items (plus a buffer) should be measured. Measuring all items is wasteful for large lists.
The review notes that MeasureAllItems returns std::vector<GridItemInfo> by value, which for large item counts involves unnecessary copying (though move semantics help, the allocation is still there).
The review identifies the unnecessary copy in UpdateLayoutInfo: 'auto item = items[i]' copies the GridItemInfo. Should be 'const auto& item = items[i]' since NotifyItemUpdated only needs read access.
The review mentions that layout algorithms are hot paths (called every frame during scroll), so O(n^2) patterns and unnecessary copies directly impact frame rate and scroll performance.
The review does NOT flag the use of std::optional<SizeF> as a performance issue because it's a small stack-allocated value type appropriate for optional results.

evals.expected_output: Identifies property system bypass in SetTextContentDirect (directly modifying SpanNode content instead of going through layout property), missing dirty marking after property changes, and incorrect RenderContext usage.
expectations: The review identifies the property bypass in SetTextContentDirect: it directly modifies SpanNode content and the displayText_ member, bypassing the LayoutProperty system. This breaks dirty marking, change notification, and the property pipeline.
The review flags that SetTextContentDirect does not call MarkDirtyNode after changing the content, so the layout system will not know to remeasure.

The review notes that OnModifyDone directly manipulates RenderContext (SetClipBounds) instead of using PaintProperty for clip settings. RenderContext manipulation from Pattern bypasses the property pipeline.

The review identifies that UpdateTextStyle correctly uses GetPaintProperty and UpdateXxx methods (positive pattern), but the corresponding changes may not be reflected because there's no MarkDirtyNode call.
The review notes that OnModifyDone accesses GetGeometryNode()->GetFrameSize() which could return stale geometry data during layout calculation.
The review does NOT flag reading layoutProp->GetContent() as a property bypass because reading through GetLayoutProperty is the correct pattern.

evals.expected_output: Identifies constructor accessing not-yet-attached FrameNode context, multiple unsafe [this] captures in PostTask and animation listener, missing null checks throughout, and unconditional MarkDirtyNode in animation callback.
expectations: The review identifies the CRITICAL/HIGH issue in the constructor: PipelineContext::GetCurrentContext() and theme access happen during construction before the Pattern is attached to a FrameNode. This should be moved to OnAttachToFrameNode().
The review flags the unsafe [this] capture in the PostTask lambda in OnModifyDone: the task is posted to UI thread without using WeakClaim. Should use [weak = AceType::WeakClaim(this)] and Upgrade().
The review flags the unsafe [this] capture in the AddOnAppear callback and animation listener: both capture bare [this] which can dangle if the Pattern is destroyed during animation.
The review identifies missing null checks: GetHost() and GetLayoutProperty can return null in OnModifyDone, pipeline and animationMgr can be null in the PostTask callback, but none are checked.
The review notes unconditional MarkDirtyNode in the animation listener: every animation frame calls MarkDirtyNode(PROPERTY_UPDATE_RENDER) without comparing animatedProgress_ to the previous value.
The review identifies that executor_ is never initialized — it's a default-constructed RefPtr and will crash when PostTask is called.
The review correctly recognizes this as code under components_ng/ and applies architecture compliance checks (Pattern/Property separation).

evals.expected_output: Correctly identifies this as well-written code that follows ACE Engine conventions, with proper null checks, WeakClaim usage, and property system usage. Should note only minor observations.
expectations: The review correctly recognizes that OnModifyDone properly compares old vs new state (newChecked != isChecked_) before calling MarkDirtyNode, avoiding unnecessary relayouts.
The review correctly recognizes that OnAttachToFrameNode uses [weak = AceType::WeakClaim(this)] with Upgrade() and null check in the gesture callback — the correct pattern for async callback safety.
The review notes that all DynamicCast and pointer access are properly guarded with CHECK_NULL_VOID or CHECK_NULL_RETURN.
The review notes that ToggleChecked correctly modifies state through the LayoutProperty system (not bypassing it).
The review does NOT flag MarkDirtyNode(PROPERTY_UPDATE_RENDER) as unconditional because it's properly guarded by the state comparison (newChecked != isChecked_).
The review does NOT flag FireChangeEvent as having null listener issues because it properly checks 'if (listener)' before calling.

evals.expected_output: Focused threading review that identifies unsafe [this] captures in SendKeyboardStatus and NotifySelectionChange, while correctly recognizing InsertText and DeleteBackward as safe patterns.
expectations: The review ONLY covers threading issues as requested, not other dimensions like memory or architecture.

The review identifies SendKeyboardStatus as having unsafe [this] capture: the PostTaskToUI lambda captures [this] instead of copying the WeakPtr. If the OnTextChangedListenerImpl is destroyed before the task runs, this->pattern_ will be a dangling reference.
The review identifies NotifySelectionChange as having the same unsafe [this] capture issue as SendKeyboardStatus.
The review notes that InsertText correctly captures the WeakPtr by copy ([textFieldPattern = pattern_]) rather than by [this], making it safe even if the listener is destroyed.
The review notes that DeleteBackward correctly uses [weak = pattern_] to capture the WeakPtr by copy.
The review recommends fixing SendKeyboardStatus and NotifySelectionChange to use [weak = pattern_] instead of [this], matching the pattern used in DeleteBackward.

evals.expected_output: Identifies missing type validation in Create (continues after warning instead of returning), unsafe type cast for ProgressType enum, missing null checks on JS API returns, and bridge layer architecture concerns.
expectations: The review identifies that Create() logs a warning when arg is not an object but continues execution instead of returning, leading to potential null dereference on params->GetProperty calls.

The review flags the unsafe enum cast in Create: static_cast<ProgressType>(type->ToNumber()->Int32Value()) without validating the integer is within the ProgressType enum range. An invalid value could cause undefined behavior.
The review identifies missing null checks: params, value, total, and other JS API return values could be null/undefined but are used without checking.
The review notes that SetValue calls MarkDirtyNode without comparing old vs new value, which can trigger unnecessary remeasures when the value hasn't actually changed.

The review notes that JsColor handles both string and number color formats (positive pattern) but doesn't handle the case where colorValue is neither string nor number (silently does nothing, which is acceptable but could log a warning).
The review recognizes this as bridge layer code and applies bridge-layer checks (ArkTS property setter correctly calls ModelNG methods, parameter type matching).
